rubric.target_patient: A postoperative early breast cancer patient with histologically confirmed ER+/HER2- resected invasive disease, no evidence of metastasis, who has completed definitive locoregional treatment (surgery with or without radiotherapy, with or without neo/adjuvant chemotherapy) and falls into an intermediate-high or high risk-of-recurrence category, and who did not achieve a pathological complete response if given neoadjuvant therapy. This is a long-horizon adjuvant endocrine therapy candidate, not a metastatic or treatment-naive patient.
rubric.site_burden: No schedule of assessments was provided, so burden is inferred from phase, design and outcomes rather than a protocol-derived visit calendar. This is a Phase III, open-label, randomized, multi-arm adjuvant trial with a planned 7-year treatment duration per arm and follow-up to 14 years for the primary endpoint (invasive breast cancer-free survival), implying the site must sustain long-term patient contact, adherence and retention infrastructure far beyond a typical short-course oncology trial. Oral drug administration across five comparator/experimental agents (camizestrant, tamoxifen, anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane) plus an abemaciclib option implies a need for dispensing, accountability, and multi-arm randomization logistics. The large enrollment target (5500 across 798 sites) suggests moderate-to-heavy per-site screening and enrollment throughput expectations, though no explicit per-site target is given.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist or breast cancer specialist capable of managing adjuvant endocrine therapy decisions in ER+/HER2- early breast cancer.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy code lists the physician under Medical Oncology, Hematology & Oncology, or a breast oncology subspecialty.
criteria.derived_from: population-defining eligibility: histologically confirmed ER+/HER2- early-stage resected invasive breast cancer

criteria.requirement: Demonstrated familiarity with endocrine therapy and CDK4/6 inhibitor use in early breast cancer, evidenced by scholarly contribution.
criteria.satisfies_if: At least one PubMed-indexed publication authored by the physician on endocrine therapy, SERDs/SERMs, aromatase inhibitors, or CDK4/6 inhibitors in breast cancer.
criteria.derived_from: interventions: camizestrant, tamoxifen, anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane, abemaciclib

criteria.requirement: The practice must see a meaningful volume of post-surgical ER+/HER2- early breast cancer patients at intermediate-high or high recurrence risk to plausibly identify and enroll eligible patients.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports an active caseload of resected ER+/HER2- early breast cancer patients meeting intermediate-high/high risk criteria, or Medicare claims data shows a floor volume of breast cancer diagnosis/treatment visits consistent with an active breast oncology practice.
criteria.derived_from: population-defining eligibility: completed definitive locoregional treatment, intermediate-high or high risk of recurrence

criteria.requirement: Site needs an established referral relationship with breast surgeons and radiation oncologists to capture patients promptly after definitive locoregional therapy, before recurrence risk staging becomes stale.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician or site self-reports participation in a multidisciplinary breast cancer program or tumor board with surgical/radiation oncology referral pathways.
criteria.derived_from: inclusion: completed adequate (definitive) locoregional therapy (surgery with or without radiotherapy), with or without (neo)adjuvant chemotherapy

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to retain and follow patients through a 7-year treatment period and up to 14 years of follow-up for the primary endpoint.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site self-reports established long-duration trial retention processes (e.g., dedicated long-term follow-up coordination, patient tracking systems) suitable for multi-year adjuvant trials.
criteria.derived_from: planned duration of treatment in either arm will be 7 years; primary outcome timeframe up to 14 years

criteria.requirement: Site must be equipped to dispense, track, and monitor adherence for multiple oral agents across randomized arms.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site self-reports pharmacy or drug accountability infrastructure for oral investigational and comparator agents.
criteria.derived_from: interventions: camizestrant, tamoxifen, anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane, abemaciclib — all administered orally

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to operationally manage an open-label, multi-arm randomized design spanning five comparator regimens plus an optional abemaciclib add-on.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician or site self-reports prior experience running open-label randomized trials with multiple comparator arms, or is listed as investigator on a comparable multi-arm study in clinicaltrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: study design: open-label randomized study comparing camizestrant vs standard endocrine therapy (AI or tamoxifen), with abemaciclib option

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as site investigator on a Phase III (or other late-phase) breast cancer trial, given the scale and registrational nature of this study.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as PI or sub-investigator on at least one completed or ongoing Phase 2/3 breast cancer trial in clinicaltrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Phase III design, 5500 planned enrollment, 798 global sites

criteria.requirement: A track record of receiving industry clinical research payments suggests established capacity to conduct sponsor-funded oncology trials.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research-category payments to the physician from a pharmaceutical sponsor in oncology.

criteria.requirement: Demonstrated GCP-compliant conduct on prior large, industry-sponsored, multi-site oncology trials similar in scale to this 798-site study.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as an investigator on at least one completed industry-sponsored oncology trial registered on clinicaltrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: 798 sites (148 US), industry sponsorship, large-scale multi-site design

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status ≤1 — a per-patient fitness check at enrollment, not a property of the physician's practice.
Adequate organ and bone marrow function — a per-patient lab eligibility threshold checked at screening, unrelated to site qualification.
Known LVEF <50% with heart failure NYHA Grade ≥2 (exclusion) — a per-patient cardiac safety exclusion applied at enrollment.
Currently pregnant or breastfeeding (exclusion) — a per-patient reproductive status check at enrollment, not a site-level criterion.
